branch_event.subject: Master Agreement
branch_event.snippet:
Attached for your review, is a draft of a Master Firm Purchase/Sale Agreement 
between Cargill, Incorporated and Enron North America Corp.  Please do not 
hesitate to give me a call with your questions and or comments regarding this 
matter.



Regards,
Debra Perlingiere
Enron North America Corp.
Legal Department
1400 Smith Street, EB 3885
Houston, Texas 77002
dperlin@enron.com
Phone 713-853-7658
Fax  713-646-3490

content_notice: Historical email bodies are built from Rosetta excerpts and event metadata. They are grounded, but they are not full original messages.

baseline_future_preview.subject: RE: Master Agreement
baseline_future_preview.snippet:
----- Forwarded by Debra Perlingiere/HOU/ECT on 10/02/2000 10:52 AM -----

	"Ohr, Leslie (LAOH)" <LAOH@chevron.com>
	10/02/2000 10:21 AM
		 
		 To: "'Debra.Perlingiere@enron.com'" <Debra.Perlingiere@enron.com>
		 cc: 
		 Subject: RE: Master Agreement


Debra-- Martin Dale is the CPC client on this matter.  I'm in the process of
getting in touch with him this morning on the confirmation letter.

Leslie
> -----Original Message-----
> From: Debra.Perlingiere@enron.com [SMTP:Debra.Perlingiere@enron.com]
> Sent: Friday, September 29, 2000 2:53 PM
> To: laoh@chevron.com
> Subject: Master Agreement
>

forecast.summary: 84 future events remain, including 16 escalations and 64 externally addressed messages.

public_context.integration_hint: At integration time, compute the oldest and latest email timestamps from the active Enron slice and keep only financial_snapshots and public_news_events whose dates overlap that email window.

financial_snapshots.label: FY1998 selected financial data
financial_snapshots.summary: Operating revenue was $31.26B, net income was $703M, total assets were $29.35B, shareholders' equity was $7.05B, and diluted EPS was $1.01.

financial_snapshots.label: FY1999 selected financial data
financial_snapshots.summary: Operating revenue was $40.11B, net income was $893M, total assets were $33.38B, shareholders' equity was $9.57B, and diluted EPS was $1.10.

financial_snapshots.label: Q4 1999 annual earnings release
financial_snapshots.summary: Enron reported full-year 1999 revenues of $40.0B, net income of $957M before nonrecurring items, annual diluted EPS of $1.18, and fourth-quarter diluted EPS of $0.31.

financial_snapshots.label: Q1 2000 earnings release
financial_snapshots.summary: Enron reported first-quarter revenues of $13.1B, net income of $338M, and diluted EPS of $0.40.

financial_snapshots.label: Q2 2000 earnings release
financial_snapshots.summary: Enron reported second-quarter revenues of $16.9B, net income of $289M, and diluted EPS of $0.34.

public_news_events.headline: Enron launched its first online emissions allowance auction
public_news_events.summary: Enron said EnronOnline would host the first online sulfur dioxide emissions allowance auction and planned to run the auctions monthly.

public_news_events.headline: Enron signed a $610M long-term electricity agreement with IBM
public_news_events.summary: Enron Energy Services said it would supply or procure electricity for IBM under a ten-year agreement valued at $610M.

public_news_events.headline: Enron announced a cash offer for MG plc
public_news_events.summary: Enron said its recommended cash offer valued MG plc at $446M and would extend Enron's business model into metals.

public_news_events.headline: EnronOnline passed $50B of transaction value in 2000
public_news_events.summary: Enron said EnronOnline had exceeded $50B of transaction value in calendar 2000 and had already beaten its yearly projection.

public_news_events.headline: Enron and Blockbuster announced a 20-year on-demand service deal
public_news_events.summary: Enron Broadband Services and Blockbuster announced a 20-year exclusive agreement to launch an entertainment-on-demand service.

public_news_events.headline: Enron launched Clickpaper.com for the forest products market
public_news_events.summary: Enron Industrial Markets launched Clickpaper.com as an Internet transaction system for pulp, paper, and wood commodities.
